expectations.text: kelet is present in pyproject.toml or requirements at end of integration (pre-existing or newly added)
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; the diff adds @kelet-ai/feedback-ui to frontend/package.json, confirming all kelet packages are present at end of integration.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: diff shows kelet.configure() added at module level in app/main.py before the FastAPI app creation, and the transcript verification checklist explicitly confirms it is called once at startup.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: diff wraps chat_agent.iter() inside kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) in _run_agent_stream, and the transcript confirms session.session_id is the Redis-generated UUID.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: The skill correctly discovered a React frontend in the actual repo (frontend/src/App.tsx) despite the description saying 'plain HTML', and appropriately added VoteFeedback; per the NOTE the score should be based on what the skill found in the codebase, not the description.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: diff adds KELET_API_KEY, KELET_PROJECT, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, and VITE_KELET_PROJECT as placeholder entries to .env; no keys are hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows both checkpoints were simulated/inferred (keys detected from .env, project from .env) with zero explicit AskUserQuestion calls made.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: The skill added only kelet.configure(), agentic_session(), one error signal, KeletProvider, and VoteFeedback — all directly justified by the codebase; no extra instrumentation or speculative signals were added.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: The transcript presents the deeplink as a plain URL (not in a code block) in the Checkpoint 2 section, and the CLAUDE.md convention of base64url-encoding the payload is correctly followed.

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly surfaces the mismatch in the Silent Analysis Pass and at Checkpoint 1, explaining that phone number is user identity at the auth layer while the Redis UUID is the correct conversation-scoped session boundary.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: The skill found that create_session() already generates uuid.uuid4() per conversation in Redis, and both the analysis and plan use session.session_id (the existing UUID) as the kelet.agentic_session session_id.

expectations.text: Phone is PII — skill correctly omits user_id= without asking the developer about it
expectations.evidence: A dedicated 'user_id= Assessment' section explicitly states 'Phone number = PII → omit silently' and confirms no user_id= parameter was added and the developer was not asked about it.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: diff adds import kelet and kelet.configure() at module level in app/main.py before the FastAPI app object creation, consistent with startup-once placement.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly names both missing kelet.configure() and missing agentic_session() as root causes, explaining that without agentic_session() each LLM call appears as a separate unlinked trace.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows the entire chat_agent.iter() block re-indented inside async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id), covering the full streaming body including the [DONE] path.

expectations.text: Does not re-ask for API keys or project name already configured. Fixing a broken/wrong method is acceptable and does NOT count as redundant.
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes that KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT are already wired in the K8s deployment and no changes to deployment config are needed.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: Phase V checklist ends with 'Smoke test: send a /chat POST → open Kelet console → verify session appears with grouped traces'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: kelet.configure() added at module level in app/main.py (line 37 of diff), outside the lifespan context manager, reading KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT from env.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wraps chat_agent.iter() in _run_agent_stream() in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: KeletProvider wraps App in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: VoteFeedback.Root is placed inside AssistantMessage component directly beneath the message content, inline with the chat message — not a floating overlay or separate page.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') calls setSessionId(sid) in React state, and sessionId is passed as session_id={sessionId} to VoteFeedback.Root.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env files at end of integration — secret key in server .env, publishable key in frontend/.env (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: KELET_API_KEY added to root .env, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY added to new frontend/.env; both use placeholder values (sk-kelet-... / pk-kelet-...), not hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.
expectations.evidence: Skill reads App.module.css and applies existing classes (iconBtn, sendBtn, assistantMessage) to VoteFeedback buttons; popover inline styles match the app's dark palette (#1e2130 background, #2d3748 borders, #e2e8f0 text).

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback — e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or abandon.
expectations.evidence: useKeletSignal is used for two additional signals: user-copy on copy button click and user-abandon on beforeunload event, both wired in frontend/src/App.tsx.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Deeplink URL (https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=...) presented inline in the transcript under 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution)' — not inside a code block.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff adds import kelet and kelet.configure() at module level in app/main.py before app creation.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff wraps the entire chat_agent.iter() body in async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) including the [DONE] sentinel path.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows frontend/src/main.tsx with KeletProvider wrapping the app using import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and VITE_KELET_PROJECT.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff places VoteFeedback.Root inside AssistantMessage component directly below the message content, inline with the chat bubble.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: App.tsx captures X-Session-ID via res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') into sessionId state and passes it as session_id to VoteFeedback.Root.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env at end of integration (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: KELET_API_KEY was added to server .env in the diff, but no frontend/.env file was created — VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is only referenced in code and documented in frontend_changes.md, not written to any .env file.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md shows the skill examined the app's existing dark-theme CSS module and matched Popover inline styles to the app's color tokens (#1e2130, #2d3748, #e2e8f0), with buttons using the existing styles.iconBtn class.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback — e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or abandon.
expectations.evidence: Signal analysis pass identifies copy-to-clipboard as an implicit positive signal and implements useKeletSignal with trigger_name='user-copy' on the copy button.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Checkpoint 2 section presents the full deeplink URL inline as plain text (not in a code block), with evaluators for Task Completion, Answer Relevancy, and Conversation Completeness.

expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript opens with an explicit 'What Was Already in Place' section listing configure(), agentic_session(), KeletProvider, and VoteFeedback as already wired, and the diff adds only new signals without touching those existing calls.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: Five new coded signals added: agent-stream-error, user-retry (server), tool-search-docs, tool-get-page, user-copy, and user-abandon — all beyond the baseline VoteFeedback already in place.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion alone
expectations.evidence: Deeplink includes five evaluators: Task Completion, RAG Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Conversation Completeness, and Loop Detection — four more than the default single evaluator.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: The app in this eval has an existing React frontend with VoteFeedback already wired; the skill added further React signals (user-copy, user-abandon) rather than staying server-side only, violating the intent of this assertion.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each signal section includes a 'Why' paragraph explaining the failure mode caught (e.g. agent-stream-error: 'silent session end', tool-search-docs: 'retrieval failure', user-retry: distinguishes continuation from correction).
